methods.description: Collection and identification of six strains of marine diatom species used in the study.

steps.description: Diatom species were supplied by Dr. Kanako Naito, Associate Professor of Hiroshima Prefecture University, Japan.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain diatom species from specified supplier

steps.description: Asteroplanus karianus was isolated in June 2014 from Tsugaru Strait, Hokkaido, Japan.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate A. karianus from Tsugaru Strait

steps.description: Thalassionema nitzschioides, N. longissima, and Skeletonema sp. strains were isolated in June 2015 from Nanaehama, Hokkaido, Japan.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate T. nitzschioides, N. longissima, Skeletonema sp. from Nanaehama

steps.description: Ditylum brightwellii was isolated in March 1989 from Hiroshima Bay, Japan.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate D. brightwellii from Hiroshima Bay

steps.description: Chaetoceros didymus was isolated in June 2015 from Nanaehama, Hokkaido, Japan.

methods.description: Preparation of reagents used in the experiments, including pH adjustment and buffer preparation.

steps.description: Deionized water with a resistivity of 18.2 MΩ was used for all experiments.
Sodium hydroxide and hydrochloric acid were used for the pH adjustment of reagents and medium.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust pH using NaOH and HCl

steps.description: HEPES was used as a buffer reagent in culture medium.
steps.searchable_summary: add HEPES as buffer in culture medium

steps.description: Sodium dihydrogen phosphate and disodium hydrogen arsenate heptahydrate were used as the phosphate and arsenic sources, respectively, in the culture medium.
steps.searchable_summary: add phosphate and arsenic sources to culture medium

methods.method_name: Preculture and Maintenance of Marine Microalgae
methods.description: Procedure for maintaining marine diatom species in nutrient medium and preparing them for experiments.

steps.description: Marine diatom species were maintained in f/2 based nutrient medium in natural sea water.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain diatom species in f/2 nutrient medium

steps.description: Culture medium and apparatus were sterilized at 121 °C for 30 min in an autoclave, followed by UV irradiation for 20 min.
Cultures were maintained in the same medium for 1-2 weeks until they reached an exponential growth phase.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain cultures until exponential growth phase

methods.description: Analysis of diatom growth and arsenic speciation under various temperature and salinity conditions.

steps.description: Diatom cultures were placed into vessels with sterilized culture medium and incubated at various temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate diatom cultures at different temperatures

steps.description: Artificial sea water at various salinities was used to culture the diatom species for the salinity treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: culture diatoms in artificial sea water with varying salinities

steps.description: As(V) and PO4 3− were added to the culture medium.
steps.searchable_summary: add As(V) and PO4 3− to culture medium

steps.description: Liquid samples were collected at specified days and filtered using 0.45 µm cellulose membrane filters.
Growth of each species was measured spectrophotometrically and calculated with a cell density-to-absorbance ratio.
Cell numbers were counted using a digital microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: count cell numbers with digital microscope

steps.description: Growth rate per day was calculated using a specific equation.
